macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Plunges Below $75K Amid Strait of Hormuz Closure and Iran-US Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin dropped below $75,000 following the full closure of the Strait of Hormuz and escalating US-Iran conflicts, triggering $237 million in long position liquidations in 24 hours. The Fear & Greed Index hit extreme fear at 21, with market cap at $2.53T down 0.81% daily but up 5% weekly.

items.title: Ethereum Dips Below $2,350, Faces Bearish Trend Amid Broader Market Pullback
items.summary: Ethereum fell 2.37% to below $2,300, losing the $2,350 level and consolidating around $2,250 with a bearish trend line forming. US spot ETH ETFs saw $275.8 million in weekly inflows despite the dip.

items.title: LayerZero Suffers $290M Hack Attributed to North Korean Lazarus Group
items.summary: The LayerZero protocol via Kelp DAO was exploited in a sophisticated attack blamed on North Korea's Lazarus Group, resulting in $290 million losses in the DeFi sector. This contributes to heightened hack fears impacting crypto prices.

items.title: c8ntinuum (CTM) Leads Gainers with 91% Surge, Followed by Enjin Coin at 25%
items.summary: c8ntinuum (CTM) topped 24-hour gainers with +91.05% to $0.22, while Enjin Coin (ENJ) rose 25.22% amid a generally down market. Other notable performers include SUSHI (+2.5%), TON (+1.49%), and CHZ (+4.60%) in SocialFi.

items.title: SocialFi and AI Sectors Buck Downtrend with Gains Amid Macro Pressures
items.summary: SocialFi rose 1.92% with TON and CHZ leading, while AI gained 0.95% led by PIEVERSE (+77.43%), contrasting broader declines in DeFi (-2.24%) and Layer 2 (-2.28%). Market dip linked to US-Iran clashes.

items.title: Michael Saylor Adds to Bitcoin Holdings Signaling Bullish Stance
items.summary: Bitcoin advocate Michael Saylor purchased additional BTC, indicating optimism despite geopolitical fears pulling prices down. BTC spot ETFs recorded $996.4M net inflows.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Mythos AI Model Sparking Security Alarms in Finance
items.summary: Anthropic's new Mythos model, in limited release, was demonstrated by UK researchers to exploit systems with weak security, prompting finance leaders to warn it could disrupt banking infrastructure. A US security agency is reportedly using it despite a blacklist.

items.title: Elon Musk Teases Grok Plugins for Microsoft Office Suite
items.summary: xAI's Elon Musk announced upcoming Grok plugins for Excel, Word, and PowerPoint, with a demo showcasing its capabilities for productivity tasks.

items.title: AWS Launches Amazon Bio Discovery for AI-Driven Drug Development
items.summary: Amazon Web Services introduced Amazon bio discovery, a new tool to accelerate AI-powered drug development processes.

items.title: Stanford AI Index: China Nearly Closes US Performance Gap Despite Lower Spending
items.summary: Stanford’s latest AI Index report reveals China has almost matched US AI performance while spending 23 times less, with AI adoption surpassing PC and internet rates.
items.source: The Next Web; Search Engine Journal

items.title: Google in Talks with Marvell for Custom AI Inference Chips
items.summary: Google is negotiating with Marvell Technology to develop two new custom AI inference chips, diversifying beyond Broadcom suppliers.
items.source: The Next Web; The Business Standard

items.title: Nvidia CEO Warns of DeepSeek on Huawei Chips as US Threat
items.summary: Nvidia's Jensen Huang cautioned that DeepSeek running on Huawei chips represents a 'horrible outcome' for the US amid ongoing export tensions and chip sales to China.

items.title: India Launches AI Chatbots to Combat Digital Arrest Scams
items.summary: CJI Surya Kant will launch the ‘Abhay’ chatbot, and CBI introduces a new AI chatbot to help verify notices and fight digital arrest frauds.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Capcom's Pragmata Hits 1 Million Sales in Two Days
items.summary: Capcom's new sci-fi shooter Pragmata has achieved strong commercial success, selling 1 million copies within its first two days of release, establishing itself as a critically acclaimed new IP.

items.title: Windrose Pirate Game Dominates Steam with 1 Million Sales in Six Days
items.summary: The early access pirate-survival game Windrose has become the second best-selling game on Steam by revenue, selling 1 million copies in six days and maintaining over 220,000 concurrent players.

items.title: Nintendo Opens New London Pop-Up Store
items.summary: Nintendo has quietly launched a 'Nintendo Experience Zone' pop-up store within Argos on Tottenham Court Road in London.

items.title: Hades 2 Launches on PlayStation 5 and Xbox with New Update
items.summary: Hades 2 is now available on PS5 and Xbox consoles, accompanied by a major update that makes it easier to romance a popular character across all versions.

items.title: Forza Horizon 6 Pre-Orders Exceed 500,000 Units
items.summary: Pre-orders for Forza Horizon 6 are performing exceptionally well, surpassing 500,000 units sold.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Vercel Internal Systems Breached via Compromised Third-Party Tool
items.summary: Vercel disclosed that its internal systems were accessed through a compromised third-party AI tool, following a claim by a ShinyHunters user on BreachForums. The cloud development platform provided additional details post-initial report.

items.title: SK hynix Begins Mass Production of 192GB SOCAMM2 for Nvidia Vera Rubin
items.summary: SK hynix announced mass production of a next-gen LPDDR5X low-power DRAM module, the 192GB SOCAMM2, designed specifically for Nvidia's Vera Rubin AI servers.

items.title: Tesla Launches Robotaxi Service in Dallas and Houston with Low Availability
items.summary: Tesla expanded its Robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston, but data shows 0% to 2% availability over the past 24 hours with only brief spikes.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: ServiceNow Beats Q2 Guidance with Strong Subscription Revenue Growth
items.summary: ServiceNow reported Q2 results exceeding guidance, with subscription revenue up 21.5% to $3.11bn and operating margin at 29.5%. The firm acquired data.world to enhance its platform.

items.title: IBM Q2 Revenue Climbs 8% Driven by Infrastructure and Software
items.summary: IBM's Q2 revenue rose 8% to $17.0bn, led by 14% growth in Infrastructure and 10% in Software, though shares fell 5% after-hours.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops in a unified computing push.
